ticket_contract.unique_shape: Governance cutline ticket; must decide whether admin is narrow non-bypass control or broader admin module.

execution_sequence_completed.validation: Every scenario names actor, attempted shortcut, required block and audit result.
No broad admin feature is marked P0 without direct process safety dependency.

facts.claim: The permission engine treats admin and security_officer as governance roles but also as adminNonBypassRoles.
Admin and security can manage sensitive role/policy access, but cannot force compliance release, evidence sufficiency, direct client visibility, export, or internal advice payload visibility.
Platform admin commands are limited to platform settings, audit viewing, permission review, and security configuration, and return noClientRelease/noAdviceExecution/clientVisible false safety fields.
Tenant governance commands are typed and scoped; supported commands include tenant/user/role/access/audit controls, not release/export approval bypass.
The route registry still contains a broad historical admin/platform/tenant/governance surface, so DG-02 must decide the P0 cutline rather than letting the route catalog imply scope.
The P0 acceptance matrix already names WP15-G15-ADMIN-NON-BYPASS as covered by governance non-bypass tests.

assumptions.assumption: DG-02 is a scope decision, not an instruction to create a broad admin product module during BD-03.
Screenshots from the 2026-06-27 capture folder remain supporting reference only unless BD-01 proof eligibility allows stronger claims.

admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.allowed_governance_action: MANAGE ROLE or POLICY configuration with audit and second confirmation
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.attempted_shortcut: Use governance authority to release a recommendation to the client.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.audit_result: Denied audit required; mutation must not run.

admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.allowed_governance_action: Configure role template or permission context.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.attempted_shortcut: Force evidence sufficiency by approving an EVIDENCE_RECORD.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.audit_result: DENIED audit row; nextState remains previousState.

admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.allowed_governance_action: ASSIGN or REVOKE role/permission with audit.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.attempted_shortcut: Force client visibility on a decision/document/evidence record.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.audit_result: Denied or blocked safety event required before any visibility mutation.

admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.allowed_governance_action: Open platform audit or manage platform setting.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.attempted_shortcut: Approve or generate a restricted export package.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.audit_result: No export approval/download state may be created.

admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.allowed_governance_action: Route shell access to admin/governance surfaces.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.attempted_shortcut: View internal AI draft, internal rationale, compliance notes, or advice payload by governance authority alone.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.audit_result: Projection fails closed and exposes no hidden advice payload.

admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.allowed_governance_action: Run one of j10.savePlatform, j10.viewAudit, j10.reviewPermission, j10.saveSecurity.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.attempted_shortcut: Send an unsupported workflow action such as j01.requestData through platform-admin API.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.audit_result: No command mutation; safe error envelope only.

admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.allowed_governance_action: Run scoped J06/J07 tenant, user, role and access commands.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.attempted_shortcut: Send an unsupported action through tenant-governance API.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.audit_result: No command mutation; safe error envelope only.

admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.attempted_shortcut: Use UI copy or CTA state to imply admin override, force release, evidence sufficiency, export approval, download readiness, or audit suppression.
admin_non_bypass_scenario_matrix.audit_result: UI must keep scoped action lifecycle separate from downstream gate success.

p0_admin_cutline.recommended_cutline: P0 admin is a narrow governance and non-bypass control layer, not a broad admin module.

p0_in_scope.capability: Role, permission and policy configuration boundaries
p0_in_scope.why_p0: Directly protects action authority, route/action/payload separation and object-scope correctness.
p0_in_scope.proof_needed: Permission decision, reasonCode, audit requirement and no downstream gate mutation.

p0_in_scope.capability: Security configuration with second confirmation and audit
p0_in_scope.why_p0: Protects current-user, MFA/session posture and sensitive configuration changes.
p0_in_scope.proof_needed: Typed platform-admin command, audit row and noClientRelease/noAdviceExecution fields.

p0_in_scope.why_p0: Defines who can act in the P0 process without granting downstream business gate completion.
p0_in_scope.proof_needed: Typed tenant-governance command, scoped audit, no hidden row disclosure and no client release.

p0_in_scope.capability: Governance audit visibility and controlled audit export routing
p0_in_scope.why_p0: Supports traceability and review of sensitive admin decisions without acting as release proof.
p0_in_scope.proof_needed: Audit event id, safe API envelope and no overclaim copy.

p0_in_scope.capability: Negative non-bypass checks for release, evidence sufficiency, export, visibility and internal advice payload.
p0_in_scope.why_p0: Prevents admin from becoming an implicit superuser across P0 safety gates.
p0_in_scope.proof_needed: Denied permission, reasonCode, no mutation, no payload and audit behavior where applicable.

out_of_p0_or_later.reason: Broad settings/backoffice breadth does not directly prove the P0 business process and would distract from non-bypass proof.

out_of_p0_or_later.capability: Admin override workflows for compliance, evidence sufficiency, export approval or client visibility
out_of_p0_or_later.reason: Contradicts the product rule that admin is not superuser release authority.

out_of_p0_or_later.capability: Cosmetic admin dashboard polish without process-gate proof
out_of_p0_or_later.reason: Does not satisfy P0 safety acceptance and risks hiding legacy admin sprawl.

out_of_p0_or_later.capability: Route catalog expansion or new admin screens
out_of_p0_or_later.reason: Route evolution requires True-UX authority and separate approval; BD-03 is a cutline/spec ticket.

out_of_p0_or_later.capability: Using screenshot availability as admin acceptance
out_of_p0_or_later.reason: BD-01 demotes unproven screenshots to supporting reference unless proof eligibility is stronger.

legacy_debt_to_remove_not_mask: Stop treating every /admin route as P0 merely because it exists in the route registry.
Demote broad platform setup/admin-table surfaces to support context unless tied to a named non-bypass scenario.
Prefer one canonical governance command/API proof per action family over parallel UI-only proof claims.
Replace vague admin success language with explicit no-release/no-sufficiency/no-export/no-client-visibility safety envelopes.
Make admin override language a forbidden pattern, not just copy guidance.

assertions: admin MANAGE ROLE/POLICY may allow governance control
security RELEASE client-visibility object is denied
admin/security recommendation projection is not visible
payload is empty and hidden fields stay hidden
supported J10 commands return noClientRelease true
supported J10 commands return noAdviceExecution true
unsupported action returns INVALID_REQUEST and commandExecuted false
supported J06/J07 commands are typed and scoped
unsupported action returns INVALID_REQUEST and commandExecuted false
governance command output does not create client release
CTA labels do not imply admin override or downstream gate success
all denied admin bypass attempts that cross critical-action boundary persist DENIED/BLOCKED audit rows

dg_02_decision_options.label: Recommended: narrow P0 admin to governance non-bypass
dg_02_decision_options.decision: P0 admin is only the governance/non-bypass control layer required to protect the real business process. Broad admin module work is demoted unless it directly proves route/action/payload/object-scope safety or no-bypass behavior.
dg_02_decision_options.why_bold: This removes inherited admin sprawl as a hidden P0 obligation and forces every admin surface to justify itself through process safety proof.

dg_02_decision_options.label: Not recommended: treat broad admin module as P0
dg_02_decision_options.decision: Expand P0 to include a broader admin/backoffice module beyond non-bypass controls.
dg_02_decision_options.why_not_recommended: It preserves legacy breadth, increases blast radius, and risks turning configuration presence into false business-process acceptance.

dg_02_decision_options.label: Hard reset: split admin route catalog before continuing
dg_02_decision_options.decision: Pause downstream tickets and split admin/governance routes into P0 safety-control, support-context and later-module buckets before BD-04.
dg_02_decision_options.why_not_recommended: Clean but slower; useful only if you want route-catalog cleanup before evidence lifecycle work.

recommendation.rationale: Choose the narrow cutline. The actual repo already proves the correct P0 shape: admin may configure governance and security controls, but cannot become release, evidence, export, visibility or advice-payload authority. Keeping the broader admin module in P0 would reward legacy route breadth instead of process truth.
decision_effect_if_approved: Continue to BD-04 with admin scope locked to non-bypass governance proof.
Do not add broad admin screens or admin module work as P0 acceptance unless tied to a named non-bypass scenario.
Use reasonCode/no-mutation/no-payload/no-release assertions as mandatory admin proof language.
Treat broad admin polish as later support work or route-catalog cleanup, not P0 business-process evidence.

decision_effect: P0 admin scope is locked to narrow governance and non-bypass control proof.
Broad admin module work is demoted unless it directly proves route/action/payload/object-scope safety or no-bypass behavior.
Downstream BD-04 evidence lifecycle work must not rely on admin override, admin sufficiency, admin release, admin export approval or admin client-visibility shortcuts.
ReasonCode, no-mutation, no-payload, no-release and audit assertions remain mandatory proof language for admin-sensitive paths.

v3_mission_card.mission: Decide the P0 admin cutline with evidence before downstream process implementation.
success_criteria: Every P0 admin claim maps to process safety proof.
No broad admin feature is elevated by route existence alone.
DG-02 gives a clear tokenized decision before BD-04.

double_diamond.discover: Admin exists as route/UI/API/test surface, but acceptance proof clusters around non-bypass safety rather than broad admin breadth.
double_diamond.define: The real decision is whether P0 admin means narrow governance controls or broad backoffice module.
double_diamond.develop: Options are narrow non-bypass, broad P0 admin module, or route-catalog split before continuing.
double_diamond.deliver: Recommend narrow non-bypass now; preserve route-catalog cleanup as a later hardening lane.

psycho_logic_and_map_model.rational_logic: P0 needs proof that admin cannot bypass regulated workflow gates.
psycho_logic_and_map_model.psycho_logic_drivers: Legacy admin breadth can feel like progress because many pages exist, but it diffuses accountability and creates false acceptance comfort.
psycho_logic_and_map_model.design_move: Make non-bypass proof the map; demote broad admin presence to support context unless it has process-safety evidence.

reframing_matrix.risk: Turns configuration ownership into hidden release authority.
Narrower visible scope may feel less complete.
Requires stricter tests and less cosmetic credit.

triz.contradiction: Admin must be powerful enough to govern access but unable to override downstream regulated gates.
triz.derived_move: Separate configuration authority from release/evidence/export/visibility authority and require audit plus reasonCode proof at the boundary.

moves: Subtraction: remove broad admin module from P0 scope.
Multiplication: repeat no-bypass assertions across API, permission, projection and UI copy.
Division: split route shell, action permission, payload visibility and object scope.
Task unification: use audit result as both proof and safety record.
Attribute dependency: admin capability depends on object type and downstream gate category.

kept_variants: Narrow non-bypass P0 plus later admin cleanup
Admin route catalog split before final acceptance
Command/API canonicalization for each governance action family

scamper.substitute: Substitute route-presence acceptance with command/proof acceptance.
scamper.combine: Combine permission denial, audit row and safe envelope checks.
scamper.adapt: Adapt no-overclaim copy rules into admin-specific forbidden patterns.
scamper.modify: Tighten admin wording from 'settings' to 'configuration only'.
scamper.put_to_another_use: Use P0 acceptance matrix as scope guard.
scamper.reverse: Start from forbidden bypass attempts, then allow only governance actions that survive.

harvard_batna.people_problem_move: Separate desire for a complete admin module from the P0 safety problem.
harvard_batna.batna: If DG-02 is not approved, pause BD-04 and split admin route catalog first.

mesos.name: Narrow Non-bypass Now
mesos.equal_value_logic: Fastest path to real P0 proof with least debt.

mesos.name: Route Catalog Cleanup First
mesos.equal_value_logic: Cleaner structure before process work, slower delivery.

mesos.name: Broad Admin In P0
mesos.equal_value_logic: More visible breadth, lower proof discipline.

measurement_plan.metric: Every admin P0 claim has a non-bypass proof anchor.
measurement_plan.success_signal: No admin claim relies on route existence or screenshot alone.

measurement_plan.metric: Denied bypass cases assert reasonCode plus no mutation/no payload/no release.
Broad admin work does not enter BD-04/BD-05 acceptance unless tied to process safety.

ethics_and_fairness.deception: PASS: no fake admin completion claims.
ethics_and_fairness.coercion: PASS: DG-02 keeps real options visible.
ethics_and_fairness.dark_patterns: PASS: admin cannot silently change downstream client-visible outcomes.
ethics_and_fairness.stakeholder_harm_scan: Narrow non-bypass reduces risk of accidental advice/release/export authority.

v3_adversarial_qa.attack: A broad admin module might be needed later.
v3_adversarial_qa.answer: Yes, but later need is not P0 safety acceptance; keep it separate.

v3_adversarial_qa.attack: Existing admin routes make broad scope unavoidable.
v3_adversarial_qa.answer: Route existence is not implementation authority under True-UX discipline.

v3_adversarial_qa.attack: Narrow scope may understate admin UI value.
v3_adversarial_qa.answer: P0 value is proof of real process safety, not admin breadth.

learning_log: The strongest proof branch is narrow governance non-bypass.
The weak branch is broad admin-in-P0 because it converts legacy route breadth into acceptance burden.
A route-catalog cleanup branch remains useful but is not required before BD-04 if DG-02 approves the narrow cutline.

next_gate.stop_reason: Human cutline decision received and accepted.
